channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Dark and Darker Season 9 Revamps Rogues, Rangers, and More
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Ironmace has revealed the release date for Dark and Darker Season 9, and it's packing new perks and abilities for Rogues and Rangers, plus a rework for one of the game's environments and more.

For Rangers, new Explosive Shot and Overdraw abilities will add a sting to your arrows, and you can also look forward to new perks like First Aid, which should allow you to administer some emergency healing to a teammate in distress.

Rogues, meanwhile, will get the new Shadow Step ability, allowing them to seemingly teleport to the other side of locked doors. Nobody, it seems, is safe from a skulking Rogue. Of course, Rogues will get new perks as well, including an update to the Creep perk.

In addition to new perks and abilities, Dark and Darker's Forgotten Castle's third floor is getting a rework; it's been "completely redesigned", according to Ironmace, so you can expect a totally new experience if you delve that far.

The Squire to Riches game mode has also been reworked "into an intense battle royale experience", and you'll find new quality-of-life upgrades like a ping system and a Party Finder menu "to improve teamwork and matchmaking", too.

If you're not familiar with Dark and Darker, it's a PvPvE dungeon-crawler in which you and your squad must explore a dungeon, find loot, and deal with enemy players who are looking to do the same thing.

Of course, you might also know the game from its developer's protracted legal battle with Nexon, which began with a DMCA takedown in 2023 and concluded with a Korean court ruling that Ironmace infringed on Nexon's trade secrets. Oops.

Dark and Darker Season 9 launches on April 28th. The game is available to check out right now on PC via Steam Early Access, so give it a look if you're after an extraction shooter with a distinctly medieval flavor.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/dark-and-darker-season-9
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 2.8 Lands in May, Steam Release Coming Soon
item.content:encoded:
Developer, publisher, and all-round gacha pusher HoYoverse has announced the release date for Zenless Zone Zero Version 2.8, and there's good news for those wishing for a Steam release as well.

Version 2.8, which, as you can probably reasonably surmise, follows on from Version 2.7, will be arriving across all platforms on May 6th. It's the last chapter of the game's second season and sees the investigation around the mysterious Ramiel deepening.

Two new characters are joining the fray in Version 2.8, the first of which, Promeia, is an S-Rank Ice Anomaly fighter who can use her special attack to automatically trigger a Perfect Dodge when she's attacked. If your squad has another Support or Anomaly agent, she can boost Abloom damage for your team as well.

Your second new character for this update is Starlight - Billy (and yes, that's how HoYoverse writes it too), the fully-upgraded version of Billy. He's got a combat motorbike and plenty of physical damage in store for enemies as well.

Both of these characters will make their debut in Version 2.8, alongside reruns of Ether Support character Lucia and Fire Attack fighters Orphie & Magus. HoYoverse doesn't say exactly when you can expect these reruns to land.

You'll also find a brand new Vortex combat mechanic in Zenless Zone Zero Version 2.8; when an enemy is affected by the Wind Anomaly, adding other attributes will now trigger Vortex, which deals AoE Anomaly damage matching the element you've thrown in.

That's what's coming in Version 2.8, but there's another treat in store too: HoYoverse has announced that Zenless Zone Zero will be making its way to Steam sometime in the future. Sadly, there's no word on whether the studio's other gacha titles will do the same.

Zenless Zone Zero Version 2.8 launches on May 6th. The game is available to play for free right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/zenless-zone-zero-version-28-steam
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Changes Direction With "New North Star" and New Priorities
item.content:encoded:
New Xbox heads Asha Sharma and Matt Booty have publicly shared an open letter they sent to Xbox employees recently, wherein a new direction for the Xbox brand aimed at building "a global platform that connects players and creators everywhere".

In the open letter, Sharma and Booty acknowledge that "players are frustrated" with various things that have been going wrong at Xbox, including infrequent console updates, a weak PC presence, and pricing that's "getting harder for people to keep up with".

It's hard to disagree with that last point; in October last year, Xbox raised the prices of its Game Pass subscription service, only to lower them once again earlier this week, so it's difficult for people to know where they stand with a Game Pass membership.

According to Sharma and Booty, the "new north star" for Xbox will be daily active players, which presumably means they'll be measuring the success of their new strategy by just how many people are logging on and playing Xbox games each day, wherever that may be.

There are four core pillars of the two execs' new direction for Xbox: hardware, content, experience, and services. On the hardware front, Xbox is pledging that its upcoming Project Helix will "lead in performance and play your console and PC games", and that the ninth console gen will be "healthy and high-quality".

When it comes to content, Sharma and Booty say Xbox will grow its "enduring portfolio", as well as building "creator-centric platforms like Minecraft, The Elder Scrolls, and Sea of Thieves". Experience-wise, Xbox wants to "fix the fundamentals" and "overhaul discovery, customization, social, and personalization".

Finally, when it comes to services, Xbox is looking to "fortify Game Pass with clear differentiation and sustainable economics", a likely jab at the service's current confusing structure of pricing and tiers.

Recent leaks suggest that Xbox isn't exactly putting its money where its mouth is when it comes to cleaning up Game Pass, though. Supposedly, a new Starter Edition is being planned as part of a partnership with Discord, and it'll further limit the games available to subscribers.

If that tier coexists with the already-in-place Xbox Game Pass Core, it's going to get even more confusing for those looking to sign up. Here's hoping Xbox can find a way to navigate the choppy waters of its new strategy.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/xbox-new-direction-post
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
